title: Man Fights Tiny Woman

jokes.text: Yeah. They're all pretty good, with one notable exception. [pause] Maria Sofia.
jokes.setup: Discussion of how great all the actors are for the show
jokes.punchline: The dramatic pause before naming the one bad actor
jokes.explanation: Effective use of pause and deadpan delivery to create comedy from what should be a diplomatic conversation

jokes.text: Extended bag-carrying battle between Larry and tiny female driver Miriam
jokes.setup: Driver offers to carry bags as part of service
jokes.punchline: Larry's awkward insistence on carrying them himself becomes increasingly uncomfortable
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry social awkwardness escalating into physical comedy. Well-structured with clear escalation and character-driven motivation

jokes.text: but I hurt my back because they sent this tiny woman driver, I was uncomfortable with her carrying my bags, and I schlepped those. Now I gotta go to a chiropractor.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to be chivalrous by not letting the driver carry his bags
jokes.punchline: His chivalry backfires and hurts him physically
jokes.explanation: Good ironic consequence of Larry's social anxiety. Sets up the episode's central conflict

jokes.text: You're being accommodating. If you're gonna do that shit, you might as well put her in the backseat and you fucking drive.
jokes.setup: Leon commenting on Larry's bag-carrying situation
jokes.punchline: Takes the logic to an absurd extreme
jokes.explanation: Leon's characteristic blunt escalation of Larry's social dilemma to absurd conclusion

jokes.text: Although I do like to have sex with women. I don't know if that makes me a sexist. Does that... Does wanting to have sex with women make you a sexist?
jokes.setup: Larry defending himself against sexism accusations
jokes.punchline: His defense completely derails into inappropriate territory
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic - trying to defend himself but making it infinitely worse. Genuinely absurd reasoning

jokes.text: I was once on the phone with a woman from Ticketmaster for half an hour, and I met her, and it was... It was ridiculous, it was crazy!
jokes.setup: Larry trying to explain how voices can be deceiving on the phone
jokes.punchline: The specific, relatable example of being disappointed by a Ticketmaster representative's appearance
jokes.explanation: Relatable observational humor with Larry's characteristic oversharing and inappropriate honesty

jokes.text: You gonna tap that? - Are you nuts? Tap what? - Come on, you hittin' that.
jokes.setup: Leon assumes Larry is pursuing the phone representative romantically
jokes.punchline: Larry's genuine confusion about what Leon is talking about
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast between Leon's assumptions and Larry's obliviousness

jokes.text: Motherfucker need a crane. You feel me?
jokes.setup: Jonas the roofer saying he's going up on the roof
jokes.punchline: Leon's blunt assessment of Jonas's size requiring heavy machinery
jokes.explanation: Leon's characteristic blunt observation delivered with perfect economy

jokes.text: Ah, the juicy tomato. Just because Tom Brady avoids them...
jokes.setup: Reading a magazine question about life-changing purchases
jokes.punchline: Larry's oddly specific answer about tomatoes and random Tom Brady reference
jokes.explanation: Typical Larry non-sequitur connecting random cultural references to mundane topics

jokes.text: If it were me, I wouldn't have done it. But that's because I lost chivalry, uh, after the divorce. - Why? What do you mean? - My wife cheated on me. - Really? - Yeah, three times.
jokes.setup: Doctor understanding Larry's chivalrous bag-carrying
jokes.punchline: The casual revelation that his wife's multiple affairs killed his chivalry
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with escalating reveals. The matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier

jokes.text: I lost chivalry, uh, after the divorce. Why? What do you mean? My wife cheated on me. Really? Yeah, three times.
jokes.setup: Doctor explaining his understanding of Larry's situation
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of being cheated on three times
jokes.explanation: Dark humor through casual delivery of painful personal information

jokes.text: Oh, Harrison Ford is a patient? No, it was a hypothetical. If Harrison Ford were a patient, I would tell you he's incredibly lovely. Bit of a curmudgeon sometimes, but... - So he's a patient? - Nope, didn't say that.
jokes.setup: Doctor using Harrison Ford as hypothetical patient example
jokes.punchline: The doctor clearly revealing Ford is actually a patient while denying it
jokes.explanation: Standard joke about someone revealing confidential information while pretending they're not

jokes.text: Well, you just told me. You just told me he's a patient. No, I didn't. I never said... I never said I've seen my patient, Harrison Ford, after his last plane crash.
jokes.setup: Larry pressing about Harrison Ford
jokes.punchline: Doctor accidentally confirming by denying specific details
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - doctor digging himself deeper by providing more specific denials

jokes.text: I know, as a Jew, I get a little rumbly in my tumbly. That's why I never became a basketball player.
jokes.setup: Discussion about anti-inflammatory medications and Jewish digestive issues
jokes.punchline: The completely unrelated conclusion about basketball career
jokes.explanation: Absurd non-sequitur that makes no logical sense but has quotable quality

jokes.text: And I get a glimpse of his underwear. It was completely frayed, like loose violin strings. And holes in it. It was like the underwear had been to war.
jokes.setup: Larry describing the doctor's underwear situation
jokes.punchline: The vivid metaphors comparing underwear to violin strings and war veterans
jokes.explanation: Creative and vivid metaphors that paint a disgustingly funny picture. Sets up major episode storyline

jokes.text: Oh, I'm a Jew for Jesus.
jokes.setup: Jason explaining he can't work Thursday afternoons because of church
jokes.punchline: The revelation that this very Jewish-looking actor is a Jew for Jesus
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy in the reveal. The setup makes the punchline unexpected and funny

jokes.text: his parents are a big ball of Jewish. He's screaming Jew.
jokes.setup: Larry describing Jason's Jewish appearance
jokes.punchline: His blunt, visual description of obvious Jewishness
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry ethnic observation. The visual 'screaming Jew' is perfectly crude and accurate.

jokes.text: The Gentiles are liking him. They'll put that one in the front. They're proud of that. - 'We took him in.' You know? - Yeah. Yeah, yeah. - 'We got one.' - We got one. - We got one. - We got one.
jokes.setup: Discussion of how Christians treat Jews for Jesus
jokes.punchline: The repeated 'We got one' becomes increasingly funny through repetition
jokes.explanation: The escalating repetition and the insight into religious group dynamics makes this work well

jokes.text: You know, it's unbelievable, the contempt that people have for you when you order anchovies. It's like I'm a pornographer or something.
jokes.setup: Waiter's reaction to Larry wanting anchovies on Caesar salad
jokes.punchline: Larry's over-the-top comparison to being a pornographer
jokes.explanation: Great observational humor with Larry's characteristic exaggeration and persecution complex

jokes.text: Hey, you're at 23 percent. I'm at two. Can I take over the charger? No. Why?
jokes.setup: Phone charging etiquette situation
jokes.punchline: Jeff's flat refusal despite Larry's clear need
jokes.explanation: Modern social dilemma - phone battery hierarchy and selfishness

jokes.text: I kind of feel like you and your friend have a strange dynamic that I'd rather not get involved with.
jokes.setup: Larry asking waitress to unplug Jeff's phone without Jeff's permission
jokes.punchline: Waitress's diplomatic way of saying she won't enable Larry's passive-aggressive behavior
jokes.explanation: Smart outside perspective on Larry and Jeff's dysfunction delivered with perfect professional diplomacy

jokes.text: Listen, we're not at the bottom of the ocean sharing one tank of oxygen. That's suicide. We'll both die.
jokes.setup: Jeff suggesting they share the charger back and forth
jokes.punchline: Larry's extreme life-or-death metaphor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - phone battery sharing as maritime disaster survival scenario

jokes.text: Look, you're the reminder, I'm the remindee. Once the reminder takes on remindership, you own it.
jokes.setup: Larry blaming Jeff for not reminding him about parking
jokes.punchline: Larry's invented terminology and logic about reminder responsibility
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry-ism with invented words and twisted logic. The 'remindership' concept is perfectly quotable

jokes.text: Like if you saw a picture of a bearded lady in the circus without the beard, and she was really attractive. Would you try and date her and convince her to shave?
jokes.setup: Leon describing his mind wandering during sleepless night
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific and inappropriate hypothetical question
jokes.explanation: Perfectly bizarre Leon logic that captures his unique thought process

jokes.text: The fuck? I had one more left. Who the fuck ate my last fucking Haagen-Dazs popsicle?
jokes.setup: Leon discovering missing popsicle
jokes.punchline: Explosive reaction to dessert theft
jokes.explanation: Good Leon character moment - disproportionate anger over small item

jokes.text: When food's missing, suspicions invariably turn to the heavyset.
jokes.setup: Leon accusing the fat roofer of eating his popsicle
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact observation about prejudice toward heavy people
jokes.explanation: Perfectly crafted observational humor with Larry's characteristic deadpan wisdom

jokes.text: That motherfucker is F-A-T positive. You know what I'm saying? If a hat was missing, I would blame a bald head motherfucker like you.
jokes.setup: Leon explaining his logic about blaming the roofer
jokes.punchline: His invented 'F-A-T positive' terminology and turning the logic on Larry
jokes.explanation: Leon's twisted logic and wordplay, plus the personal insult to Larry makes it effective

jokes.text: You know what I'm saying? If a hat was missing, I would blame a bald head motherfucker like you.
jokes.setup: Leon explaining his fat-blaming logic
jokes.punchline: Applying same logic to Larry's baldness
jokes.explanation: Great extension of Larry's logic with personal insult - Leon's perfect comeback

jokes.text: Send him up. You go up and you supervise from down here. I'm the roofer's helper. He goes up, I stay down here.
jokes.setup: Heavy roofer going up, assistant staying down
jokes.punchline: Complete reversal of logical work distribution
jokes.explanation: Good workplace absurdity - wrong person doing dangerous work while helper stays safe

jokes.text: I don't think he really wants to be a roofer. I'm looking into tattooing.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Eric going on roof
jokes.punchline: Eric's career change to completely different manual profession
jokes.explanation: Mild character comedy. The switch from roofing to tattooing shows young person career confusion.

jokes.text: Yeah. Same shit that come out of a lady after she have a baby.
jokes.setup: Leon describing the pimiento in olives
jokes.punchline: His deeply inappropriate and gross comparison
jokes.explanation: Shockingly inappropriate Leon observation that's funny because of how unnecessarily graphic it is

jokes.text: Your underwear, it's threadbare beyond all normal usage. You gotta have some underwear awareness.
jokes.setup: Larry confronting the doctor about his frayed underwear
jokes.punchline: The concept of 'underwear awareness' as a life skill
jokes.explanation: Great callback to earlier underwear observation with Larry's invented concept of 'underwear awareness'

jokes.text: Once the cotton detaches from the elastic, they gotta go. - And are your balls dangling? - They are. Once your balls are dangling, it's over.
jokes.setup: Larry giving underwear replacement advice
jokes.punchline: The escalating graphic specificity about when underwear becomes unwearable
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the underwear theme with increasingly graphic and specific criteria

jokes.text: Once the cotton detaches from the elastic, they gotta go. And are your balls dangling? They are. Once your balls are dangling, it's over.
jokes.setup: Larry giving underwear replacement rules
jokes.punchline: Increasingly graphic criteria for disposal
jokes.explanation: Escalating underwear rules with increasingly graphic detail - effective gross-out comedy

jokes.text: A heroin addict doesn't keep the needles.
jokes.setup: Doctor asking about emergency underwear use
jokes.punchline: Drug addiction comparison for underwear disposal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant analogy - comparing underwear disposal to drug addiction recovery with perfect economy

jokes.text: A heroin addict doesn't keep the needles.
jokes.setup: Doctor asking if he should keep his bad underwear for emergencies
jokes.punchline: Larry's comparison to drug addiction recovery
jokes.explanation: Brilliant analogy with perfect economy. Takes underwear discussion to unexpectedly profound territory

jokes.text: I am Bjorn, son of Bjorn, and we make fish. That is what my family does. I am the greatest fisherman in the whole sea.
jokes.setup: Seth Rogen in full Viking costume trying to do his lines
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous Viking dialogue delivered while clearly uncomfortable in costume
jokes.explanation: Mildly amusing fish of place moment but relatively simple physical comedy

jokes.text: I am Bjorn, son of Bjorn, and we make fish. I am the greatest fisherman in the whole sea.
jokes.setup: Seth attempting Viking character
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous Viking dialogue with modern actor discomfort
jokes.explanation: Silly Viking dialogue contrasted with Seth's obvious discomfort in costume

jokes.text: I'm not... They don't want me to carry my bags. They said it's sexist. I had a whole conversation with them. Sure you did.
jokes.setup: Larry making same excuse about not carrying bags
jokes.punchline: Seth's sarcastic disbelief
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the opening bag situation, showing Larry's learned nothing

jokes.text: Oh, no. It's just, people get pregnant by God all the time.
jokes.setup: Jason defending the virgin birth as believable
jokes.punchline: Larry's sarcastic response about divine pregnancy frequency
jokes.explanation: Sharp sarcastic response that undercuts religious logic with Larry's characteristic bluntness

jokes.text: He went next door and he spoke to his friend, he said, 'She's... She's out of her fucking mind. I gotta get out of there.'
jokes.setup: Discussing Joseph's reaction to Mary's pregnancy announcement
jokes.punchline: Larry's modern, profane interpretation of Joseph's likely response
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of religious story with realistic modern reaction

jokes.text: You got a toddler carrying your shit around still? Yeah, that was, uh, slightly embarrassing. It's a bad look. I know that.
jokes.setup: Seth referencing the small female driver
jokes.punchline: 'Toddler' description emphasizing the absurd optics
jokes.explanation: Good callback with escalated description - 'toddler' makes the visual even more absurd

jokes.text: They may like an everyman, but most every man is kind of stupid. Exactly, that's how I come across. I portray myself as stupid so people relate to me more.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Seth's everyman persona
jokes.punchline: Seth admitting he deliberately appears stupid for relatability
jokes.explanation: Insightful observation about celebrity persona management with good back-and-forth

jokes.text: So you're a smart person who's trying to appear stupid so people will like you? Yeah, and you're a nice person appearing to be an asshole so no one likes you?
jokes.setup: Larry understanding Seth's strategy
jokes.punchline: Seth's perfect counter-observation about Larry
jokes.explanation: Brilliant mutual character analysis - Seth perfectly diagnoses Larry's social strategy with perfect parallel structure

jokes.text: You don't want a man face to face, but you want a man to stick his finger up your fuckin' ass? You're gonna feel comfortable with a strange woman sticking her finger up your ass?
jokes.setup: Larry's contradictory preferences for male vs female medical professionals
jokes.punchline: Seth pointing out the logical inconsistency in graphic terms
jokes.explanation: Good logical deconstruction of Larry's contradictions with appropriately crude language

jokes.text: A very bright, enterprising woman who wants to make dicks and butts better.
jokes.setup: Larry questioning what kind of woman becomes a urologist
jokes.punchline: Seth's matter-of-fact, crude but accurate description
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response that reduces medical profession to crude but accurate terms

jokes.text: It was like ancient underwear. It was like rustic. It was like underwear that was from the Pilgrims or something.
jokes.setup: Freddy asking about not returning to the doctor
jokes.punchline: Larry's escalating historical metaphors for old underwear
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of underwear descriptions - historical references make it funnier

jokes.text: Physical fight between Larry and tiny female driver over bags
jokes.setup: Same bag-carrying conflict from beginning
jokes.punchline: The situation escalates to actual physical struggle
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and payoff to episode's central conflict. The visual of Larry fighting a tiny woman is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Look, Mommy. Mommy! That man is beating up that woman. No, she... She won't let me carry... They're my bags!
jokes.setup: The physical struggle over the bags
jokes.punchline: Child's innocent interpretation vs Larry's desperate explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect outside perspective that shows how Larry's behavior appears to innocent observers

jokes.text: Remind me never to drive you again. Oh, don't worry. I own that remindership. You will never ever have to think about that for the rest of your life. That's on me.
jokes.setup: Driver's sarcastic parting shot
jokes.punchline: Larry using his earlier 'remindership' terminology to accept responsibility
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to the remindership concept, showing Larry learned his lesson about taking responsibility

jokes.text: Hey! Hey! Hey! Let it go, motherfucker. Let it go. - Come here, come here. - Let it go, you fat bitch.
jokes.setup: Leon confronting Jonas about stealing his charger
jokes.punchline: Physical altercation with Leon's crude insults
jokes.explanation: Payoff to the stolen food subplot but relatively straightforward physical comedy

jokes.text: Oh my God! Holy fuck! That's not a good look. I'm not working with that guy.
jokes.setup: Leon fighting with the roofer
jokes.punchline: Larry's reaction focusing on optics rather than the fight
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry reaction - concerned about professional reputation during physical altercation

jokes.text: Larry, they were so grateful that I upgraded the underwear. I'm so happy for you. And I can't thank you enough.
jokes.setup: Doctor's waiting room now full of patients
jokes.punchline: The underwear advice actually worked and saved his practice
jokes.explanation: Good resolution to the underwear subplot showing Larry's advice actually helped

jokes.text: I would love to tell you about our Lord and Savior, Jesus Christ. I have pamphlets. Oh, fuck.
jokes.setup: Larry's success with the underwear advice
jokes.punchline: Doctor now converted to Christianity and proselytizing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback and escalation - Larry's helpful advice backfires spectacularly as doctor becomes Jew for Jesus

jokes.text: I would love to tell you about our Lord and Savior, Jesus Christ. I have pamphlets. Oh, fuck.
jokes.setup: Doctor expressing gratitude to Larry
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's also become a Jew for Jesus, trying to convert Larry
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and callback - Larry's good deed backfires in the worst possible way. Great episode closer

tmdb_overview: Larry navigates the complexities of requesting a new chauffeur, letting his roofer do his job, and imparting some constructive criticism to his chiropractor.
